name: Chicken & Artichoke Salad with Yogurt Dressing
intro: I do like a good grilled chicken salad, but to be honest, I’m slightly over chicken Caesar salad, as the dressing alone racks up hundreds of calories and it’s not quite the light lunch I once believed it to be. I started using yogurt dressings for salads years ago and think they are highly underrated. For the salad itself, I prefer leaves such as watercress and rocket, as these have bold flavours that add punch to the dish instead of wilting in amongst the other ingredients. And I adore preserved artichoke hearts! I can eat them with and in everything, so it’s clear why this salad became one of my favourites.

ingredients.item: artichoke hearts preserved in oil, drained and halved
packet of chives, each stem snipped into 3
packet of flat leaf parsley, leaves picked and barely chopped
packet of mint, leaves picked and barely chopped
red onions, cut in half and thinly sliced into half moons
long red chillies, thinly sliced into rings
grated rind of 2 lemons and juice of 1½

method: Preheat a griddle pan (or a frying pan, if you prefer) over a high heat. Place the chicken breasts between two layers of clingfilm and and flatten them out to an even thickness by pounding them with a rolling pin or meat mallet. Start pounding from the centre of each breast and work outwards towards the edges. Brush each flattened chicken breast with olive oil and season on both sides with sea salt and black pepper. Griddle the chicken breast for approximately 6 minutes (depending on the thickness of the breasts) on each side until they are cooked through. Remove the chicken breasts from the heat and allow to rest.
To make the dressing, combine the dressing ingredients in a small bowl with sea salt and black pepper to taste and stir well until they are evenly blended.

Put the watercress in a large mixing bowl along with the artichoke halves, walnuts, herbs, onions, chillies and just a little sea salt and black pepper. Then break the feta roughly into 2.5cm (1in) chunks and mix all the ingredients together well. Slice the chicken breasts thinly, almost shredding them, add them to the salad and toss the salad lightly until all the ingredients are evenly distributed.
Arrange the salad on a large flat serving platter, then drizzle the dressing all over the salad before serving.
